codeReview.summary: The commit adds lifecycle states, enforcement guards, migration, superadmin lifecycle endpoints, and module-gating interceptor wiring, but module gating is not actually tenant-configurable through production API paths, leaving the core feature incomplete despite the new runtime checks.

issues.description: No request field exists for tenant enabled modules, so superadmin/company update flows cannot configure `enabled_modules` at all. Combined with CompanyService update logic, module flags are never writable via API, which means per-tenant module feature gating cannot be administered in runtime as required.

`update(...)` updates name/code/timezone/quotas only and never calls `company.setEnabledModules(...)`; therefore `enabled_modules` remains effectively fixed at defaults unless changed out-of-band in DB. This prevents operational use of module gating for tenant lifecycle/admin endpoints.

`normalizeRequestedEnabledModules(...)` is present but unused by production code (only referenced in its unit test), indicating dead-path design drift and reinforcing that module-setting flow is incomplete.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture knowledge is missing the new tenant runtime conventions introduced here (gatable vs core module set and ACTIVE/SUSPENDED/DEACTIVATED enforcement semantics).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md` currently documents generic multi-tenancy/error patterns but does not record module-gating model or lifecycle runtime access rules that are now enforced in `CompanyContextFilter` and `ModuleGatingInterceptor`.

summary: Reviewed feature `module-feature-gating-and-tenant-lifecycle` using handoff, commit diff, worker transcript skeleton, and skill guidance. Lifecycle enforcement and superadmin lifecycle endpoints are implemented, but module gating is not tenant-configurable through API update contracts/service logic, so the feature does not fully satisfy module feature-gating behavior in practice.
